reasoning.typography: Open Sans + Merriweather is the 2013 Google Fonts pairing every content site defaulted to. Zero personality, pure template energy. It's legible but utterly forgettable.
reasoning.hierarchy: Headlines are doing some work (bold, sized up), but everything sits at the same rhythm. The sidebar avatar + name + links creates competing hierarchy with main content. No single dominant element per screen.
reasoning.distinctiveness: This is a Ghost template with minimal customization. Swap 'Pragmatic Engineer' for any other tech blogger and it works identically. The nav structure (Newsletter | Podcast | Guidebook | Books) is commodity.
reasoning.color: Restrained palette (grey background, dark text, subtle underlines). No accent color abuse. Professional and disciplined, though utterly safe.
reasoning.density: Uniform padding throughout. The left sidebar is spacious, articles have consistent vertical rhythm, but it's calibrated for 'blog template' not confident content density. Nothing surprising.
reasoning.voice: The structure is pure Ghost stencil: hero title, horizontal nav, sidebar with author card + links, article list with dates. The IA doesn't reflect any unique story—it's 'blog + products' like 10,000 others.

winning_patterns: Clear article headlines do most of the visual work
Consistent vertical rhythm makes content scannable
anti_patterns: Open Sans + Merriweather is the Ikea Allen wrench of web type
Ghost default template with almost zero customization
Sidebar navigation competes with article content for attention
Generic 'Newsletter | Podcast | Books' nav could be anyone's
Uniform padding/whitespace shows no opinion on density
